cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why the author mentioned Zhang Wei's example. The options are A to D. First, I need to look at the part of the text where Zhang Wei is mentioned. The passage says that Zhang Wei, known as "Tangjiasanshao", earned 177 million yuan. He was on the list of wealthiest Chinese Internet novelists and was on Forbes. The author also notes that he's at the top, but most online writers remain unknown. However, he urges that success can be achieved by others if they write hard enough. So the author uses Zhang Wei as an example of someone who achieved success through online writing. The key point here is to show that such success is possible, even if most don't make it. The author's purpose in mentioning him would be to illustrate that there are successful cases, thus showing an example of success. Looking at the options: A is to introduce another writer, but the text isn't just introducing him; it's highlighting his success. B is to show another writer's example of success.
